game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
Deceptively simple. Infinitely complex. LYNE is a minimalist puzzle game that will knot your brain as it calms your soul. Connect the shapes. Fill the board. Lose yourself in the interflowing paths of LYNE.


Deceptively simple. Infinitely complex. LYNE is a minimalist puzzle game that will knot your brain as it calms your soul.


Connect the shapes. Fill the board. Lose yourself in the interflowing paths of LYNE.

Features

  • Hundreds of pre-built puzzles
  • An infinite number of procedurally-generated puzzles, released daily
  • Slick, intuitive interface
  • Soothing dynamic audio
  • Unlockable colour palettes
  • Multiple languages supported
game_info.description: LYNE is a minimalist puzzle game where you connect matching shapes by drawing paths through the board. Each shape has a number indicating how many connections it needs. Fill all required connections to complete the puzzle. Features multiple levels with increasing complexity, elegant visuals, and a calming yet challenging experience.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move cursor between nodes
Space: Select/deselect node to start/end a path
Z: Undo last connection
R: Restart puzzle (on game over screen)
ESC: Pause/unpause game
Enter: Start game

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement and node selection mechanics. Validates that the cursor can move between nodes and that nodes can be selected and deselected properly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move cursor systematically through all nodes on the board. Select and deselect various nodes to ensure state tracking works correctly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Cursor should move smoothly between nodes. Node selection should toggle correctly. No crashes or state corruption should occur.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests winning condition by solving the puzzle optimally. Implements a solution strategy that connects all matching shapes with the correct number of connections.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Use breadth-first search to find valid paths between matching shapes. Build connections systematically, ensuring all shape requirements are met without crossing invalid paths.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: All shapes should be properly connected with required number of connections. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state. Win screen should display with final score.

TEST_2.expected: All shapes should be properly connected with required number of connections. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state. Win screen should display with final score.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests path creation and undo functionality. Validates that paths can be drawn between nodes and that the undo action properly removes the last connection.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Create several paths between nodes, then use the undo function to remove them one by one. Test edge cases like undoing with no paths and creating complex multi-node paths.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Paths should be created correctly and visible on screen. Undo should remove paths in reverse order. State should remain consistent after multiple undo operations.

TEST_3.expected: Paths should be created correctly and visible on screen. Undo should remove paths in reverse order. State should remain consistent after multiple undo operations.
